conjecture_title: Open Question on s((Z/kZ)^n) for fixed k
paper_title: Erdős-Ginzburg-Ziv constants by avoiding three-term arithmetic progressions
summary: The open question of estimating s((Z/kZ)^n) for fixed k and large n remains unresolved. For prime k=p, Fox–Sauermann (2018) established s(F_p^n) ≤ 2p·r(F_p^n), giving an exponential upper bound via Ellenberg–Gijswijt, but a gap between this and Elsholtz's exponential lower bound (base ~2.08 for odd k≥3) remains open. For composite k the Alon–Dubiner bound (cn log n)^n·k remains the state of the art, far exceeding the exponential lower bound. No subsequent verified paper closes this gap.
search_queries: Erdos-Ginzburg-Ziv constant s((Z/kZ)^n) fixed k large n bounds Fox Sauermann 1708.09100
EGZ constant (Z/kZ)^n fixed k exponential lower bound Elsholtz Alon Dubiner
"Exponential bounds for the Erdős-Ginzburg-Ziv constant" arXiv
notes: No verified post-2018 paper found that resolves or substantially narrows the gap between the exponential lower bound (base ~2.08 for odd k≥3, Elsholtz) and the upper bounds for s((Z/kZ)^n) with fixed k and large n. Naslund (arXiv:1701.04942) proved exponential upper bounds for prime-order groups before the Fox–Sauermann paper; Hegedüs (arXiv:1712.00228, 2017) extended these to arbitrary finite abelian groups; both arXiv versions predate Fox–Sauermann and are not post-2018 results. The question for composite fixed k remains fully open, with a large gap between polynomial-exponential upper bounds and exponential lower bounds.
